conversations.userText: What were the pool leak detection results for Deborah Justice in CompanyCam report?
conversations.assistantText:
**Deborah Justice – 181 Isbell Road, Fair Play, SC**
Pool volume: 37,602 gallons

**Leak Detection Findings:**
- Deep end skimmer throat: small water loss at missing grout locations
- Shallow end skimmer throat: missing grout/towels noted, but no water loss
- Spa waterline: missing grout in several areas, small amount of water loss occurring
- **Primary spa circulation line: active leak** — confirmed via dye testing, pressure testing, and acoustical location devices. Damage estimated below the valving at the filtration equipment (marked on photo)
- Main drain nearest spa: missing pool finish/coating in sump area — **actively losing water**
- Other 3 main drains: same missing coating, but no active water loss currently
- Small crack near shallow-end steps (by autofill line) — no active water loss

fields.reportFindings: -- 2 of 27 -- Significant water loss is present in the deep end skimmer throat. Missing grout is present where the tile.and grout meet the PVC skimmer body. Sealant will be required to properly resolve water loss at this location Several cracks were present on the shallow end and middle skimmer. When water level is at normal operating level a small amount of water is occurring at these areas. Prior sealant has been applied at shallow end skimmer Water loss is present at filtration equipment area. A small amount of water loss was observed at the pool pump outlet. The plumbing line threads into the pump body housing. Due to the age of the pump, it may be time to replace seals as required. conversations.userText: What were the leak detection findings for Valley View Condominiums from the CompanyCam report?
conversations.assistantText:
**Valley View Condominiums — Leak Detection Checklist (06/16/2026, Chris & Logan)**

Source: CompanyCam report "Exported - Current Aquatrace Swimming Pool Leak Detection Checklist 06-16-2026.pdf" (doc 17867388). No matching Jobber job record was returned (getJobDetail failed), so this is CompanyCam-only data.

**Findings:**
- Deep end skimmer throat: significant water loss where tile/grout meets the PVC skimmer body due to missing grout — needs sealant.
- Shallow end & middle skimmers: cracks with minor water loss at normal water level; shallow skimmer already has prior sealant applied.
- Filtration equipment area: minor water loss at pool pump outlet (plumbing threads into pump housing); recommend rebuilding pump with new seals/bearings during the plumbing repair.
- Deep end wall & other areas: cracks present but **no active water loss** — flagged as potential future issues only.
- Equipment room/heater area: condensation observed (interior and exterior wall) — **not a water loss issue**, just heater condensation.

**Result flags:**
- Structure: hard water loss found = true
- Filtration: hard water loss found = true
- Lights, plumbing, roof/solar: no water loss found

**Water loss estimate** (from linked Evaporation Calculator, doc 18142072):
- Evaporation: ~0.75"/day (451 gal/day)
- Potential leak loss beyond evaporation: ~1.75"/day (1,183 gal/day) — flagged as "strongly suggests an active leak"
- Total daily water loss: ~2.5"/day (1,635 gal/day)

conversations.userText: What were the leak detection findings for Camp Mikell from the CompanyCam report?
conversations.assistantText:
**Camp Mikell (Alex Mastej) — Leak Detection Checklist, 06/05/2026**
Source: CompanyCam doc 17663518 (Chris, Catherine — techs on site)

Water loss confirmed in multiple areas:
- **Backwash line** — ~1,700 gal/day loss
- **Main drain #3** — ~2,000 gal/day loss, plus a defect under the main drain cover in the pool structure
- **Entry corner (left)** — plaster defect, missing grout at 2 tiles, small water loss
- **Tile Defect area 1** — tiles separating from wall, missing grout, small water loss
- **Skimmer 5** — multiple tile defects, missing grout, small water loss at skimmer throat
- **Tile Defect 2** — tile/grout separating, small water loss
- **Basin at floor returns** — water loss around several floor return jet perimeters (plaster-to-fitting)
- **Equipment room** — Jandy valve for skimmers showing pressure loss at seals; Sand Filter 1 has a small leak at the multi-port valve

No loss found (defects only, no active leak):
- Entry deck — 4 cracked tiles
- Tile Defect 3 — broken tiles, no loss
- Skimmer 7 area (Tile Defect 4) — tile/grout separating, **no loss currently but high potential**

**Pool specs:** ~101,000 gallons, avg depth 56"

Note: I could not pull matching data from Jobber (getJobDetail failed to return a result), so this is CompanyCam-only — no cross-rail comparison available for this job.

**Leak Detection Findings — Valley View Condominiums** (Dillard, GA — service date 06/16/2026, techs: Chris Sears & Logan)
*Source: CompanyCam report "Exported - Current Aquatrace Swimming Pool Leak Detection Checklist 06-16-2026.pdf"*

- **Deep end skimmer throat:** Significant water loss — missing grout where tile meets PVC skimmer body; sealant required to resolve.
- **Shallow end & middle skimmers:** Cracks present causing small water loss at normal operating level; prior sealant already applied at shallow end skimmer.
- **Filtration/pump area:** Small water loss at pool pump outlet where plumbing threads into pump housing. Recommend rebuilding pump (seals/bearings) during outlet repair due to age.
- **Deep end wall & other locations:** Cracks noted but **no active water loss** — flagged as potential future issues only.
- **Equipment room/heater area:** Condensation observed (interior and exterior wall) — **not a leak**, confirmed as heater condensation only.

**Quantified loss (from Evaporation Calculator, same visit):**
- Evaporation estimate: 0.75"/day (~451 gal/day)
- Potential leak loss (beyond evaporation): **1.75"/day (~1,183 gal/day)** — flagged as "significant unexplained water loss, strongly suggests an active leak"
- Total daily water loss: 2.5"/day (~1,635 gal/day)

**Rail comparison:** No matching Jobber job data was available (getJobDetail failed) — all findings above are sourced solely from the CompanyCam rail. No Jobber-side notes to cross-check against.

conversations.userText: What were the leak detection findings for Camp Mikell from the CompanyCam report?
conversations.assistantText:
Leak Detection Checklist for Camp Mikell (Alex Mastej), performed 6/5/2026 by Chris & Catherine — source: CompanyCam doc 17663518.

**Water loss findings:**
- ~1,700 gal/day at the backwash line
- ~2,000 gal/day at main drain #3, with a defect under the main drain cover
- Entry corner: plaster defect, missing grout at two tiles, small water loss
- Entry deck: 4 cracked tiles, no water loss
- Tile Defect 1: tiles separating from wall, missing grout, small water loss
- Skimmer 5: multiple tile defects, missing grout at skimmer throat, small water loss
- Tile Defect 2: tile/grout separating, small water loss
- Tile Defect 3: broken tiles, no water loss
- Skimmer 7: multiple tile defects, tile/grout separating — **no loss yet, but high potential**
- Floor return basins: water loss around several fitting/plaster perimeters
- Equipment room: Jandy skimmer valve showing pressure loss at seals
- Sand Filter 1: small leak around multi-port valve (top and bottom half)

**Summary flags:** hard water loss confirmed in structure, plumbing, roof/solar, and filtration categories; no hard loss found in lights.

**Pool specs:** ~101,000 gallons, avg depth 56", 16 skimmers, 16 wall returns, 4 floor returns, 17 main drains, 17 lights.

Note: Jobber has no matching job-detail record for this lookup — findings above are CompanyCam-only.

**Normal priority:**
- Semrush — Site Audit — new site issues flagged on aquatraceleak.com
- InMotion Hosting — WHOIS confirmation notice for aquatraceswimmingpoolleakdetection.com
- Google Cloud — Free Trial welcome notice

**Low priority:**
- Gmail Team (x2) — inbox tips/app promo — not client related
- PayPal — legal agreement update notice

**Bottom line:** Nothing here is an active client service request — top items are internal (business brief, county research) and account/sign-in noise. Site Audit issue is worth a quick look if SEO matters to you.

conversations.userText: what ssues were found on rachel paynes pool
conversations.assistantText:
Rachel Payne's leak inspection (7/6/26, tech Chris) found the leak on the return-side plumbing:

- Pool is a vinyl liner (Buster Crabbe) with an AquaGenie combined skimmer/return system.
- Customer reported water dropping to skimmer level with system off, and a faster loss with the pump running.
- Main drain had already been shut off by others, but the loss continued through the skimmer — ruling that out.
- Testing traced the leak to the **active return line connecting to the return side of the AquaGenie blade port**. Water stopped dropping once level reached that blade area with the system off, confirming suction-side plumbing is not the issue.
- Flagged categories: **structure, plumbing, and filtration** all show hard water loss found — no issues on lights or roof/solar.

Pool holds about 18,500 gallons, average depth 58".

fields.reportFindings: -- 2 of 15 -- Rachel Payne Vinyl Liner Swimming Pool Leak Detection Report Aquatrace inspected the vinyl liner swimming pool at this property based on reported water loss. Per the customer, the pool water level drops to approximately the bottom of the skimmer area and stops when the circulation system is off. When the system is running, the pool continues to lose water at a more significant rate. The main drain had previously been suspected by others and was shut off, but water loss continued while the pool was operating through the skimmer only, although the loss was reported as less severe. This pool is a Buster Crabbe vinyl liner pool equipped with an AquaGenie skimmer system. The AquaGenie is a combined skimmer and return system. The skimmer operates normally for suction, while the return water is discharged back into the pool through a blade-style water outlet located just below the skimmer mouth. Both the suction plumbing and return plumbing connect to the AquaGenie skimmer body. Based on the testing performed, the leak appears to be associated with the active return line where it connects to the bottom/return side of the AquaGenie blade return port. The pool does not continue to drop with the water static and the system off once the water level reaches the AquaGenie return blade area. This indicates the suction-side plumbing is not the primary issue. The pump basket is l...

m4-report-extraction-1783288187771-7bca8.userText: What were the leak detection findings for Valley View Condominiums from the CompanyCam report?
m4-report-extraction-1783288187771-7bca8.assistantText:
**Valley View Condominiums — Leak Detection Checklist (06/16/2026, Chris & Logan)**

Source: CompanyCam report *"Exported - Current Aquatrace Swimming Pool Leak Detection Checklist 06-16-2026.pdf"* (doc ID 17867388). No matching Jobber job record was found for this project (getJobDetail failed), so this is CompanyCam-only data.

**Findings:**
- **Deep end skimmer throat** — Significant water loss; missing grout at tile/PVC skimmer body junction. Sealant required.
- **Shallow end & middle skimmers** — Cracks present, minor water loss at normal operating level; shallow end already has prior sealant applied.
- **Filtration/pump area** — Water loss at pool pump outlet where plumbing threads into pump housing. Recommend rebuilding pump (seals/bearings) during outlet repair due to age.
- **Deep end wall & other areas** — Cracks noted but no active water loss; flagged as potential future issues only.
- **Equipment room** — Condensation around heater and exterior wall — not a leak, just condensation.

**Structural results:**
- Structure: hard water loss found = **true**
- Filtration: hard water loss found = **true**
- Lights: **pass**, no loss
- Plumbing & Roof/Solar: not applicable, no loss

**Supporting data:**
- Pool: 1,048.9 ft² surface, 54" avg depth, ~35,402 gal
- Evaporation calc (separate doc): estimated evaporation 0.75"/day (451 gal); potential leak loss 1.75"/day (1,183 gal) beyond evaporation — "strongly suggests an active leak."

m4-report-extraction-1783288271732-f1a9e.userText: What were the leak detection findings for Valley View Condominiums from the CompanyCam report?
m4-report-extraction-1783288271732-f1a9e.assistantText:
**Valley View Condominiums — Leak Detection Checklist (06/16/2026, Chris & Logan)**

Source: CompanyCam report "Exported - Current Aquatrace Swimming Pool Leak Detection Checklist 06-16-2026.pdf" (doc 17867388). No matching Jobber job record was returned (getJobDetail failed), so this is CompanyCam-only data.

**Findings:**
- Deep end skimmer throat: significant water loss where tile/grout meets the PVC skimmer body due to missing grout — needs sealant.
- Shallow end & middle skimmers: cracks with minor water loss at normal water level; shallow skimmer already has prior sealant applied.
- Filtration equipment area: minor water loss at pool pump outlet (plumbing threads into pump housing); recommend rebuilding pump with new seals/bearings during the plumbing repair.
- Deep end wall & other areas: cracks present but **no active water loss** — flagged as potential future issues only.
- Equipment room/heater area: condensation observed (interior and exterior wall) — **not a water loss issue**, just heater condensation.

**Result flags:**
- Structure: hard water loss found = true
- Filtration: hard water loss found = true
- Lights, plumbing, roof/solar: no water loss found

**Water loss estimate** (from linked Evaporation Calculator, doc 18142072):
- Evaporation: ~0.75"/day (451 gal/day)
- Potential leak loss beyond evaporation: ~1.75"/day (1,183 gal/day) — flagged as "strongly suggests an active leak"
- Total daily water loss: ~2.5"/day (1,635 gal/day)

m4-report-extraction-1783288300056-9e6cf.userText: What were the leak detection findings for Camp Mikell from the CompanyCam report?
m4-report-extraction-1783288300056-9e6cf.assistantText:
**Camp Mikell (Alex Mastej) — Leak Detection Checklist, 06/05/2026**
Source: CompanyCam doc 17663518 (Chris, Catherine — techs on site)

Water loss confirmed in multiple areas:
- **Backwash line** — ~1,700 gal/day loss
- **Main drain #3** — ~2,000 gal/day loss, plus a defect under the main drain cover in the pool structure
- **Entry corner (left)** — plaster defect, missing grout at 2 tiles, small water loss
- **Tile Defect area 1** — tiles separating from wall, missing grout, small water loss
- **Skimmer 5** — multiple tile defects, missing grout, small water loss at skimmer throat
- **Tile Defect 2** — tile/grout separating, small water loss
- **Basin at floor returns** — water loss around several floor return jet perimeters (plaster-to-fitting)
- **Equipment room** — Jandy valve for skimmers showing pressure loss at seals; Sand Filter 1 has a small leak at the multi-port valve

No loss found (defects only, no active leak):
- Entry deck — 4 cracked tiles
- Tile Defect 3 — broken tiles, no loss
- Skimmer 7 area (Tile Defect 4) — tile/grout separating, **no loss currently but high potential**

**Pool specs:** ~101,000 gallons, avg depth 56"

Note: I could not pull matching data from Jobber (getJobDetail failed to return a result), so this is CompanyCam-only — no cross-rail comparison available for this job.

m4-report-extraction-1783288365900-fd27e.userText: What were the leak detection findings for Valley View Condominiums from the CompanyCam report?
m4-report-extraction-1783288365900-fd27e.assistantText:
**Leak Detection Findings — Valley View Condominiums** (Dillard, GA — service date 06/16/2026, techs: Chris Sears & Logan)
*Source: CompanyCam report "Exported - Current Aquatrace Swimming Pool Leak Detection Checklist 06-16-2026.pdf"*

- **Deep end skimmer throat:** Significant water loss — missing grout where tile meets PVC skimmer body; sealant required to resolve.
- **Shallow end & middle skimmers:** Cracks present causing small water loss at normal operating level; prior sealant already applied at shallow end skimmer.
- **Filtration/pump area:** Small water loss at pool pump outlet where plumbing threads into pump housing. Recommend rebuilding pump (seals/bearings) during outlet repair due to age.
- **Deep end wall & other locations:** Cracks noted but **no active water loss** — flagged as potential future issues only.
- **Equipment room/heater area:** Condensation observed (interior and exterior wall) — **not a leak**, confirmed as heater condensation only.

**Quantified loss (from Evaporation Calculator, same visit):**
- Evaporation estimate: 0.75"/day (~451 gal/day)
- Potential leak loss (beyond evaporation): **1.75"/day (~1,183 gal/day)** — flagged as "significant unexplained water loss, strongly suggests an active leak"
- Total daily water loss: 2.5"/day (~1,635 gal/day)

**Rail comparison:** No matching Jobber job data was available (getJobDetail failed) — all findings above are sourced solely from the CompanyCam rail. No Jobber-side notes to cross-check against.

fields.reportFindings: -- 2 of 24 -- A small amount of water loss is present in the deep end skimmer throat at missing grout locations Although no water loss is present, several towels are missing in the shallow end skimmer throat At normal operation level of the spa water line, several areas are missing grout. A small amount of water loss is occurring in these areas Come and dye testing along with pressure testing and acoustical location devices, water loss is occurring in primary spa circulation line. The damaged area is estimated to be below the valving at the filtration equipment as indicated in the marked up photo The swimming pool main drain nearest the spa is missing pool finish material within the main drain sump area. Water loss is occurring at this area actively. All other main drains in the swimming pool are also missing the same coating. However, there is no water loss present currently at those three locations A small crack is present in the shallow end to the left of the steps near the autofill line. No water is actively being lost at this location All other swimming pool and spa basin structure, plumbing and filtration systems systems were in proper working condition at the time of this inspection The spa light had been removed by others prior to our arrival. The damaged area is estimated to be below the valving at the filtration equipment as indicated in the marked up photo The swimming pool main drain nearest the spa is missing pool finish material within the main drain sump area. Water loss is occurring at this area actively. All other main drains in the swimming pool are also missing the same coating. However, there is no water loss present currently at those three locations A small crack is present in the shallow end to the left of the steps near the autofill line. No water is actively being lost at this location All other swimming pool and spa basin structure, plumbing and filtration systems systems were in proper working condition at the time of this inspection The spa light had been removed by others prior to our arrival. web-7e2524c5-9aca-4df2-936c-bdee3f70702b.userText: what ssues were found on rachel paynes pool
web-7e2524c5-9aca-4df2-936c-bdee3f70702b.assistantText:
Rachel Payne's leak inspection (7/6/26, tech Chris) found the leak on the return-side plumbing:

- Pool is a vinyl liner (Buster Crabbe) with an AquaGenie combined skimmer/return system.
- Customer reported water dropping to skimmer level with system off, and a faster loss with the pump running.
- Main drain had already been shut off by others, but the loss continued through the skimmer — ruling that out.
- Testing traced the leak to the **active return line connecting to the return side of the AquaGenie blade port**. Water stopped dropping once level reached that blade area with the system off, confirming suction-side plumbing is not the issue.
- Flagged categories: **structure, plumbing, and filtration** all show hard water loss found — no issues on lights or roof/solar.

Pool holds about 18,500 gallons, average depth 58".

fields.reportFindings: -- 2 of 15 -- Rachel Payne Vinyl Liner Swimming Pool Leak Detection Report Aquatrace inspected the vinyl liner swimming pool at this property based on reported water loss. Per the customer, the pool water level drops to approximately the bottom of the skimmer area and stops when the circulation system is off. When the system is running, the pool continues to lose water at a more significant rate. The main drain had previously been suspected by others and was shut off, but water loss continued while the pool was operating through the skimmer only, although the loss was reported as less severe. This pool is a Buster Crabbe vinyl liner pool equipped with an AquaGenie skimmer system. The AquaGenie is a combined skimmer and return system. The skimmer operates normally for suction, while the return water is discharged back into the pool through a blade-style water outlet located just below the skimmer mouth. Both the suction plumbing and return plumbing connect to the AquaGenie skimmer body. Based on the testing performed, the leak appears to be associated with the active return line where it connects to the bottom/return side of the AquaGenie blade return port. The pool does not continue to drop with the water static and the system off once the water level reaches the AquaGenie return blade area. This indicates the suction-side plumbing is not the primary issue. The pump basket is l...
